reasoning: The value on November 9, 2025, corresponds to the post-meeting level following the Fed's October 29 rate cut (dropping from ~4.31 to an immediate floor around 3.91, then settling at 3.93 by Nov 7). Since there are no FOMC meetings scheduled for November, the target rate will remain strictly unchanged for the Nov 16 and Dec 9 resolution dates. Daily repo market pressures often revert toward the IORB (roughly 3.90-3.92 for the new 3.75-4.00% target range). Thus, 3.93 represents a slightly elevated level within this band, meaning that near-term observations on Nov 16 and Dec 9 face natural downward regression toward the 3.91 mean, keeping the likelihood of printing *strictly greater* than 3.93 somewhat below 50% (roughly 45% and 35%, accounting for mid-month and mid-cycle repo variations). By the Feb 07, 2026 date, the Fed will have concluded its December and January FOMC meetings. Current market pricing via the CME FedWatch Tool implies a ~64% likelihood of a 25 bps cut at the December meeting alone, and overall expectations heavily skew toward at least one cut by February. In that scenario, SOFR will drop to the ~3.65-3.70% neighborhood, virtually eliminating the chance of exceeding 3.93. Factoring in a small probability of the Fed pausing its rate cuts due to persistent inflation gives us an estimated 15% chance of the rate being elevated by February.
